seoTitle: Set Up Email Notifications for Webflow Form Submissions
metaDescription: How to configure real-time email alerts for Webflow form submissions using the native Send to routing engine, no third-party tools needed.
intro: Getting notified the moment a lead fills out your contact form shouldn't require setting up webhooks or third-party automation tools. Webflow has a native email notification system built directly into each form block, letting you route submissions to specific inboxes with custom subject lines and dynamic content. Here's how to set it up.

steps.heading: Select your form block
steps.body: Open the Navigator panel on the left, expand the tree, and click your main Form Block wrapper.

steps.heading: Open Element Settings
steps.body: Click the Gear icon in the right sidebar to load the Element Settings panel for that form.

steps.heading: Add an email notification
steps.body: Scroll to the Send to section and confirm Email Notifications is active, or click the plus icon to add one.

steps.heading: Set your recipient details
steps.body: Enter your destination email in the To field, add a Sender Name, and map the Reply-to field using a form variable so you can respond directly.

steps.heading: Customize the subject and body
steps.body: Write a subject line using tokens like {{siteName}} or {{formName}}, add {{formData}} to the body to stream all responses, then click Apply and publish your site.

faq.question: Can I send notifications to more than one email address?
faq.answer: Yes, the To field supports multiple recipients so several team members can be alerted on the same submission.

faq.question: What does the {{formData}} variable do?
faq.answer: It automatically pulls in all the submitted field values so you don't have to manually list each one in the email body.

faq.question: Why aren't my notification emails arriving?
faq.answer: Check your workspace tier for any rules around notification limits, and confirm the recipient email address is entered correctly.

faq.question: Do I need to publish my site for the notification changes to take effect?
faq.answer: Yes, changes only go live once you publish, so remember to click Publish after applying your settings.
